data.title: Tech Stocks Experience Decline in Pre-Market Trading
data.description: Some prominent technology stocks showed weakness in pre-market trading. According to ChainCatcher, AMD shares fell by 1.3%, Intel experienced a decline of nearly 1%, and Micron Technology saw a drop of over 1%.

data.title: Pedro Sanchez Intensifies Efforts to Regulate Digital Platforms
data.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez has intensified his efforts to regulate digital platforms, marking the latest development in his ongoing campaign. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the increasing scrutiny these platforms face under Sanchez's administration. The move is part of a broader strategy to ensure compliance with national regulations and protect consumer rights. Sanchez's administration has been vocal about the need for stricter oversight to address concerns related to data privacy and market competition. This initiative aligns with similar regulatory trends observed across Europe, where governments are seeking to balance innovation with accountability in the digital sector. The outcome of these efforts could have significant implications for the operations of major tech companies within Spain and potentially influence broader European regulatory policies.

data.title: India Strengthens Ties with Bangladesh After Landmark Election
data.description: India is taking swift action to reinforce its relationship with Bangladesh in the wake of last week's significant election. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the importance of this diplomatic effort. The election results have prompted India to engage more closely with its neighbor, aiming to solidify bilateral ties and ensure regional stability. This move is seen as a strategic step to enhance cooperation and address mutual concerns between the two countries. The focus is on fostering economic collaboration and strengthening political alliances to benefit both nations.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Iranian Foreign Minister Engages in Talks with Oman
data.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi is currently engaged in discussions with Omani officials. According to Jin10, Araqchi entered the negotiation venue a few minutes ago and is meeting with Oman's Foreign Minister Busaidi.

data.title: Analysis: The bear market is expected to continue until mid-2027. Closely monitor the crossover signal between the 90-day and 365-day moving averages.
data.description: Crypto analyst Axel stated in a blog post that the current bear market cycle began at the all-time high of approximately $125,000 in October 2025—a fact confirmed by the Entity-Adjusted Liveliness indicator, which peaked at 0.02676 in December 2025, exhibiting a typical lag compared to price, and is now reversing downwards. Historically, such reversals typically initiate an accumulation phase lasting 1.1 to 2.5 years. The chart clearly shows the previous two accumulation cycles: the 2020 bear market lasted 1.1 years, and the 2022–2024 bear market lasted 2.5 years. Both started in exactly the same way, with the green line reversing from its peak and entering a sustained decline, followed by a price drop. The current pattern is structurally identical. If this historical pattern repeats itself, this accumulation period will last at least until the end of 2026, with a more realistic expectation of until mid-2027. The key confirmation signal is when the 90-day moving average reverses downwards and breaks below the 365-day moving average (0.02622). Until this crossover occurs, the possibility of a medium-term reset and a resumption of the upward trend remains.

data.title: Altseason About to Begin? These Charts Say Yes
data.description:
Several crypto analysts are pointing to the same conclusion: structural signals that previously marked the start of altseason may be triggering again. The discussion centers around a handful of charts shared by Crypto Patel, Bitcoinsensus, CryptoBusy, and Moustache, all highlighting long-term trend shifts in altcoin dominance and relative strength.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/altseason-about-to-begin-these-charts-say-yes/

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Pre-Market Sees Gains in Chinese Tech Stocks
data.description: In the U.S. pre-market trading session, several popular Chinese technology stocks showed strength. According to Jin10, Baidu (BIDU.O) rose by over 1%, GDS Holdings (GDS.O) increased by 0.8%, and Alibaba (BABA.N) saw a 0.7% uptick. These movements indicate a positive sentiment towards Chinese tech companies among investors.

data.title: The dollar continued its slight upward climb, ignoring market expectations of approximately three more rate cuts by the Federal Reserve.
data.description: The dollar rose slightly for the second consecutive trading day, completely ignoring market pricing in expectations of about three Federal Reserve rate cuts this year. Options markets showed that recent bearish sentiment towards the dollar has eased, with the so-called front-end risk reversal indicator falling to its lowest negative level in nearly a month. The currency market still expects the Fed to cut rates by about 64 basis points by the end of the year. Some strategists believe this expectation is excessive, as three rate cuts may exceed the reasonable range supported by data, which would expose the market to the risk of a dollar rebound. Elias Haddad, global head of market strategy at Brown Brothers Harriman, said, "Expectations of Fed rate cuts seem a bit excessive, which provides room for adjustment in the short-term strength of the dollar." He pointed out that economic growth is strong and inflation has remained above the Fed's 2% target. (Jinshi)

data.title: Kalshi's transaction volume exceeded 34.8 million in February, on track to set a new monthly record.
data.description: KalshiData stated on the X platform that as of February 14th, Kalshi had recorded over 34.8 million transactions this month, averaging approximately 2.5 million transactions per day. In comparison, Kalshi recorded approximately 54.5 million transactions in January, meaning February's transaction volume has already reached about 64% of last month's total. If this pace continues, Kalshi's monthly transaction volume is expected to set a new record, potentially exceeding 69.6 million transactions.

data.title: BFF Shares Decline Amid Investigation by Prosecutors
data.description: BFF shares experienced a decline in Milan trading following confirmation of an investigation by prosecutors into the specialist lender. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of the news on the company's stock performance. The investigation's details remain undisclosed, but the announcement has raised concerns among investors, leading to a noticeable drop in share value. The market is closely monitoring the situation as further developments unfold.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Criticizes Maryland Governor Over Pipeline Incident
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has criticized Maryland Governor Wes Moore for his delayed response to a pipeline rupture incident that occurred in Northwest Washington in January. According to Jin10, the rupture led to sewage spilling into the Potomac River. Despite the fact that the District of Columbia Water and Sewer Authority and the federal government have jurisdiction over the broken pipeline, Trump directed his criticism towards Moore.

data.title: Tariffs Impact South African Wine Prices
data.description: The imposition of tariffs on South African agricultural products by the United States is leading to increased wine prices in the country. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the economic repercussions of these tariffs on South Africa's wine industry. The tariffs, part of broader trade measures, have affected the cost structure for wine producers, resulting in higher prices for consumers. This development underscores the ongoing challenges faced by South African exporters in navigating international trade policies. The wine industry, a significant contributor to the country's economy, is experiencing pressure as it adapts to these new financial constraints. Stakeholders are closely monitoring the situation, assessing the potential long-term impacts on trade and economic growth.

data.title: Harvard Endowment Reduces Bitcoin ETF Holdings by 21%, Initiates Position in Ethereum ETF
data.description: Harvard University’s $56.9 billion endowment reduced its exposure to a spot bitcoin exchange-traded fund while initiating its first investment in an ether-focused ETF during the fourth quarter. According to CoinDesk, Harvard Management Company disclosed in a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission that it purchased nearly 3.9 million shares of BlackRock’s iShares Ethereum Trust (ETHA), marking its first reported position tied to ether. At the same time, the endowment cut its stake in the iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) by about 21%, selling roughly 1.5 million shares. Despite the reduction, IBIT remains Harvard’s largest publicly disclosed holding, valued at $265.8 million. The portfolio adjustment followed a decline in bitcoin’s price from an all-time high of around $125,000 in October to just below $90,000 at the end of the quarter. Andy Constan, founder and chief investment officer at Damped Spring Advisors, said the move may reflect market dynamics rather than a shift in sentiment. He suggested the sale could be linked to the unwinding of a strategy aimed at exploiting premiums in digital asset treasury companies, which had traded above the value of their bitcoin holdings as measured by multiple of net asset value, or mNAV.

data.title: Expert Warns UK Regulatory Delays Could Hinder Crypto Innovation
data.description: Andrew MacKenzie, CEO of a pound-pegged stablecoin firm, has expressed concerns over the slow pace of regulatory progress in the UK. According to NS3.AI, MacKenzie warns that these delays might undermine the country's ambitions to become a leader in the digital asset space. He emphasized that the lack of timely regulatory frameworks could dampen innovation and investment momentum, posing challenges for the UK as it faces global competition to establish dominance in the crypto sector.

data.title: Sweden's Finance Minister Confirms No Immediate Plans to Adopt Euro
data.description: Sweden will not be adopting the euro currency in the foreseeable future, as stated by Finance Minister Elisabeth Svantesson. Bloomberg posted on X that Svantesson emphasized the country's decision to maintain its current currency, the Swedish krona, citing economic stability as a key factor. The minister's remarks come amid ongoing discussions in Europe about currency adoption and economic integration. Sweden has consistently opted out of joining the eurozone since the euro's introduction, prioritizing national economic policies and financial autonomy. The decision reflects Sweden's cautious approach to economic changes and its commitment to evaluating the potential impacts on its economy before making any significant shifts in currency policy.
